notes: This is the end of the Keepers League. Your characters and their progress will be migrated over the next few hours following the patch, or you can use the migration options in the character selection menu. Thanks for playing!

The following items have been removed, and existing items will be deleted upon logging in: Fleshgraft, Growing Wombgift, Augmentation Implant, Exalted Implant, Regal Implant, Unstable Implant, Draining Eshgraft, Jolting Eshgraft, Replicating Eshgraft, Storming Eshgraft, Aegis Tulgraft, Freezing Tulgraft, Squalling Tulgraft, Summoning Tulgraft, Battering Uulgraft, Hardening Uulgraft, Impaling Uulgraft, Spiking Uulgraft, Erupting Xophgraft, Flamecaller Xophgraft, Molten Xophgraft, and Stoking Xophgraft.
You can now create Private Leagues for the Mirage League, ahead of the league's launch on March 6. These leagues will not begin until the Mirage League launches.
After this patch, local cached assets will be cleaned up upon launching the client.

patch_notes.patch: Content Update 3.28.0 — Path of Exile: Mirage
notes: Take on a rogue sect of the Faridun as they sap the energies of captured Djinn for nefarious ends, and help a legendary Maraketh Sekhema put an end to an ancient war once and for all.
The Afarud, the most depraved and twisted of the Faridun, have formed a pact with Saresh, a powerful necromancer who built an army of undead to seek revenge on the Maraketh.
Deny their twisted efforts towards dominion by joining forces with Varashta and freeing the spirits of Djinn before the Afarud can accomplish their goals.

You will encounter the physical form of trapped Djinn, imprisoned and tortured by Afarud Warlocks. After successfully defeating them, you will be asked by Varashta to enter The Astral Realm, a Mirage containing an imperfect copy of the world around you.

Varashta can augment the magic of the Mirage she allows you to enter, giving you the option to choose one of three Wishes before you do so. These Wishes modify the Mirage in powerful and potent ways, transforming the copy within and causing monsters slain to drop powerful loot, currency for enhancing your equipment and gems, special caches, or even strange mysterious coins.

Fight your way through the monsters, astral hazards, and Afarud mages to the tether holding the Djinn. Shatter this chain to free the Djinn, step by step weaking whatever dark plans the Afarud may have for Wraeclast.

With 3.28.0, there are Standard, Hardcore and Solo Self-Found variations of the Mirage challenge league available. They have the same core mechanics and items. You can create private league versions of these leagues, with mods that make the game harder.
The Hardcore variations of Mirage are parented to their Standard Mirage equivalent, meaning if you die in Hardcore Mirage you can continue on in Standard Mirage League.

The new Mirage challenge league includes a set of 40 new challenges, and 8 new challenges in Ruthless Mirage. Earn pieces of the Orb Regalia Armour Set when you complete 12, 20, 28 and 36 challenges in Mirage, or 2, 4, 6 and 8 challenges in Ruthless Mirage. These challenge rewards are only obtainable in this league.

From the 19th challenge onwards and for every third challenge after that, you will receive pieces of the Mirage Totem Pole decoration to display in your hideout. The Totem Pole permanently showcases how many of the Mirage challenges you completed during the league.

Added the new Reliquarian Ascendancy Class for the Scion. Notable Passive Skills for the Reliquarian borrow from the powers of Unique items found throughout Wraeclast, and will change with each League. For this reason, these Notables will not be available as outcomes for the Forbidden Flame and Forbidden Flesh Unique Jewels.
Introduced Exceptional Support Gems, a new and improved version of Awakened Support Gems, which are being phased out. There are over 40 Exceptional Support Gems, which can be found in the Endgame.
Introduced the Coin of Knowledge, which Corrupts a level 20 Skill Gem, Imbuing it with a random valid Intelligence Support effect.
Introduced the Coin of Power, which Corrupts a level 20 Skill Gem, Imbuing it with a random valid Strength Support effect.
Introduced the Coin of Skill, which Corrupts a level 20 Skill Gem, Imbuing it with a random valid Dexterity Support effect.

Added a new Strength/Intelligence Skill Gem, Divine Blast: Project a beam of holy light from your shield to a targeted location, causing an implosion of holy energy. This energy then lingers for a short duration, before blasting outwards in a larger and more damaging explosion.

Added a new Strength/Intelligence Skill Gem, Holy Hammers: Slam the ground ahead of you, calling down a Holy Hammer from above. On hitting an enemy, another Hammer will cascade forward, repeating if further enemies are hit, up to a cap. A Power Charge can be expended to call down additional Hammers to either side of the initial slam. Requires a Mace or Staff.

Added a new Strength/Intelligence Skill Gem, Holy Strike: Infuse your weapon with holy energy and swing. Successfully hitting an enemy will summon forth a Holy Armament, which copies your main hand weapon stats to determine its own. These Holy Armaments are undamageable minions, and will attack the enemies you target with Holy Strike. Requires a Mace, Sceptre or Staff.

Added a new Strength/Intelligence Skill Gem, Shield of Light: Retaliate against a blocked hit with a sweep of your Shield, unleashing a wave of light in a cone in front of you. Additional waves also emanate from allies in a radius around the first target struck, towards that target.
Added a new Strength Transfigured Skill Gem, Shockwave Totem of Authority: Summons a totem that shakes the earth around you, damaging enemies.

Added a new Strength/Intelligence Transfigured Skill Gem, Static Strike of Gathering Lightning: Attack with a melee weapon, gaining a Gathering Lightning buff for a duration if you hit an enemy. While you have Gathering Lightning, attacks with this skill will cause chaining beams of lightning, which chain farther the more Gathering Lightning you have.

Added a new Strength/Intelligence Transfigured Skill Gem, Searing Bond of Detonation: Summons a Totem that casts a beam of fire at you and each other Totem you control, dealing Burning Damage to enemies caught in the beam. Enemies near either end of a beam also suffer Burning Damage. Reaching maximum active Totem count will cause all Totems from this skill to detonate, applying the same Burning Damage for a duration.

Added a new Dexterity Transfigured Skill Gem, Charged Dash of Projection: Channel to project an illusion which you steer. You gain stages while it moves, until it stops at a maximum total distance. Waves of area damage frequently pulse along its path, based on your attack speed. Stop channelling to release the illusion, dealing a final wave of damage. Requires a Melee Weapon.

Added a new Dexterity/Intelligence Transfigured Skill Gem, Flamethrower Trap of Stability: Throws a trap that releases a number of flames in different directions around it when triggered. The flames last for a duration repeatedly dealing damage.

Added a new Intelligence Transfigured Skill Gem, Kinetic Fusillade of Detonation: Wave your wand to create multiple hovering kinetic projectiles. After a short duration, the projectiles explode, dealing area damage. Using this attack again will reset the duration for all hovering projectiles. Cannot be directly used by traps or mines.

Added a new Intelligence Transfigured Skill Gem, Orb of Storms of Squalls: Creates an electrical orb that will strike enemies in its area of effect with beams of lightning when placed, as well as when you use a lightning skill while within its area. These beams of lightning can then split to hit more enemies. When striking enemies, this orb will teleport to a random enemy struck. Casting this skill again will replace the previous orb.

Added a new Intelligence Transfigured Skill Gem, Shock Nova of Procession: Casts a ring of Lightning around you, followed by a larger Lightning nova. Each effect hits enemies caught in their area with Lightning Damage. This then repeats, with each repeat offset from the previous in the direction of your target.

Added a new Intelligence/Strength Transfigured Skill Gem, Storm Burst of Repulsion: Unleash orbs of energy while you channel that repeatedly jump away from the targeted location until their duration expires, dealing damage in a small area after each jump. When you stop channelling, all remaining orbs explode, dealing higher damage in a larger area.

Added a new Intelligence/Dexterity Transfigured Skill Gem, Siphoning Trap of Pain: Throws a trap that applies debuff beams to a number of nearby enemies for a duration. The beams chill enemies and deal cold damage over time.

Added a new Strength/Intelligence Support Gem, Blessed Call Support: Supports Warcry skills, causing them to create Consecrated Ground. Supported Warcries have lowered Area of Effect and Cooldown Recovery Rate. Cannot support triggered Warcries.

Added a new Strength/Intelligence Support Gem, Excommunicate: Supports melee attacks, causing them to gain a percentage of physical damage as extra fire and lightning damage, and excommunicate enemies on melee hit. Does not modify skills used by minions.

Added a new Intelligence Support Gem, Exemplar Support: Supports Minion skills, causing your minions to have increased Critical Strike Chance and Critical Strike Multiplier for each of your own recent Critical Strikes.
Added a new Strength/Intelligence Support Gem, Hallow: Supports melee attacks you use yourself, causing them to inflict Hallowing Flame on melee hit. Does not modify skills used by minions.

Added 10 new Runegrafts that can be found by shipping to ports in Kingsmarch: Runegraft of the Agile, Runegraft of Connection, Runegraft of Consecration, Runegraft of Fury, Runegraft of the Imbued, Runegraft of Rallying, Runegraft of Resurgence, Runegraft of Rotblood, Runegraft of the Spellbound, and Runegraft of Suffering.
Added 13 new Unique Items.
Added 8 new Divination Cards.
The "Exotic" sub-stash of the Currency Stash has been split into two new sub-stashes "Atlas" and "League".
Continued to incrementally improve the sound, art, effects and environments.

Extensive changes have been made to the Atlas and how you engage with Maps, without interfering with the beloved core experience. Some of the more frustrating aspects have also been streamlined or removed.

Map items are no longer associated with a specific Map Area. As an example, instead of having a Tier 10 Mesa Map, you will now find a Tier 10 Map. To open a Mesa Map, you now locate and select Mesa on your Atlas, put in your Map item, and activate the Map Device.
That Mesa Map may drop a Tier 11 Map. You can then place this into your Map Device, which defaults to the last Map run, and either run Mesa again or choose a new Map Area to use it on.
The structure of the Atlas has also changed, you now begin your exploration at the centre of the Atlas, with each corner housing one of four Voidstone slots.
All content accessed using the Map Device is now physically present on the Atlas, allowing you to see everything available in the Endgame as you reveal the fog of war.
All Unique Maps are now present on the Atlas, including some old favourites that are appearing for the first time.

You now gain 1 Atlas Skill Point per Unique Map completed on your Atlas, up to a maximum of 10. There are around 20 Unique Maps available, so you only need to complete the half you want (not looking at you, Hall of Grandmasters).
The remaining Atlas Skill Points have been moved to other places, and you can now get more total Atlas Skill Points than before.

Eagon now has a new goal and is determined to stop Zana traversing the threads that weave the Atlas together and severing them. He has made use of a new mysterious discovery, Arcane Astrolabes, but by using them to trace her movement they cause dangerous side effects.

Using an Astrolabe on a Map on the Atlas manifests a Shaped Region in that quadrant of the Atlas, affecting many of the Maps within it. There are many types of Astrolabes, and they apply different types of Shaped Regions.
Completing the content added by the Astrolabe will remove the Shaped Region's influence from that Map. Upon doing so, every other Map remaining in the Shaped Region will gain an additional modifier.
Once you have successfully rid the Atlas of the Shaped Region, Eagon will take you to one of the four Memory Vaults, where there are powerful rewards to be had.
Each Vault is associated with a specific quadrant on the Atlas, so you're able to target a certain vault if you're wanting to try to get a specific type of reward.
Interacting with the Map Device now opens the Atlas screen. Clicking locations on the Atlas will allow you to use a Map (and any fragments) to open portals to that specific location.
Certain locations will require special map items to access, such as Unique Maps, Shaper Guardian Maps, Nightmare Maps, Vaal Temple and locations requiring Map Fragments rather than Map Items.
As you can now run any Map with a Map Item, the Favoured Map system has been removed.
Memory Skills have been removed from Originator Influenced areas. You can still collect Petals in these areas, which now instead grant a higher chance to find items with Memory Strands.

Several of the effects from the removed Memory Skills have been moved to new bonus modifiers on Altars at the end of Maps within Memory Threads. For example, Killing a Rare Monster consumes 30 Petals if possible to revive the Monster once.
The Decayed Voidstone is now obtained from killing both the Shaper and the Elder. It can also still be obtained from defeating Uber Elder.
You now receive a Voidstone from killing both the Searing Exarch and the Eater of Worlds (instead of one from each).
The extra Voidstone that this leaves is now obtained from killing the Incarnation of Dread.
Voidstones now upgrade the minimum Map Tier of Maps within that specific Atlas Quadrant to 16, cause Maps in that Quadrant to drop one Tier higher, and convert Tier 15 and lower maps found to Gold.
Each Voidstone now also comes with a special bonus that affects all Maps on the Atlas, as well as granting the ability for Nightmare Maps to drop.
Introduced the Memory of Loneliness, which adds a Memory Thread to a quadrant of the Atlas leading to the Incarnation of Neglect.
Introduced the Memory of Trauma, which adds a Memory Thread to a quadrant of the Atlas leading to the Incarnation of Fear.
Introduced the Memory of Reverence, which adds a Memory Thread to a quadrant of the Atlas leading to the Incarnation of Dread.
The Threads of the Originator Quest line now awards a skill book granting 2 Atlas Passive Skill Points and 2 Atlas Passive Respec Points for defeating each Incarnation boss.
Tier 17 Maps have undergone significant changes, and are now Nightmare Maps.
Completing each Nightmare Map now awards 1 Atlas Skill Points (5 total).
Slightly increased the reward values of Originator-influenced and Nightmare Map modifiers that granted Increased Pack Size or Item Rarity.
Slightly reduced the reward values of Originator-influenced and Nightmare Map modifiers that granted More Currency, Maps or Scarabs.

After obtaining the Voidstone from killing the Incarnation of Dread, items with Memory Strands will now be able to drop from all maps ran on the atlas. More Memory Strand affected items will still come from Originator maps, to retain them being the best source.
Items that drop with Memory Strands will now more often be higher tier bases.
Items that drop with Memory Strands will have more Memory Strands on average.
Memory Strands will have a larger influence on the tier of Modifiers present on the item than previously.
Atlas Bonus Objectives now additionally grant +1% to Map Item Quantity for every 4 Bonus Objectives completed.
Uber Bosses now only require 4 Fragments to access (previously 5).
All Maven Invitations are now accessed through Kirac, rather than some of them being quest items you place into your Map Device.

Sacrifice and Mortal Fragments can no longer be used in a Map Device to open a random Vaal Side Area directly. Instead, they can be used alongside Maps to add a Vaal Side Area to the opened Map, while still applying their current Map Item Quantity bonus.
Sacrifice and Mortal Fragments now explicitly state the Map Item Quantity bonus they apply to areas.
Elder Guardian, Shaper Guardian and Conqueror Maps always generate on Tier 16 Maps. As a result, Divination Cards for these items no longer explicitly state the Map Tier.

Blessed Orbs are now able to reroll the specific Conqueror inhabiting Conqueror Maps, or the specific Elder Guardian inhabiting Elder Guardian Maps. This change does not affect pre-existing Conqueror Maps or Elder Guardian Maps.

Kirac no longer offers Atlas Missions, and defeating Map Bossess no longer has a chance to grant Kirac Missions. As a result, the Planar Tactician, Solidarity, Expert Reconnaissance, Commissioned Officer and Expect the Unexpected Notable Atlas Passives and associated small Passives have been removed.
Scouting Reports can no longer be obtained, and existing items no longer function.
The Side Quest Divination Card has been temporarily disabled, and existing copies of this card cannot be traded in.

Kirac's Shop now sells a wider variety of Map related items, and his shop can be rerolled with Gold. Maps within Kirac's Shop generally sell for Gold, although some special items may still sell for Chaos Orbs or Orbs of Chance.

Cartographer's Chisels can no longer be obtained, though existing items will still function, and Map Device crafting options have been removed. The loss of content and loot here has been compensated in other areas.
The Cartographer Divination Card has been temporarily disabled, and existing copies of this card cannot be traded in.
Temple Architects now drop additional Scarabs instead of additional Map Currency while you have an Atlas of Worlds room in the Temple.
Reduced the chances for Quality Currency to drop from Synthesised Monsters in Synthesised Maps.
Removed all Vendor Recipes that could grant Cartographer's Chisels.
The effect of 20% Quality on Maps has been reduced. Cartographer's Chisels in Standard Leagues now provide +10% Item Quantity at 20% Quality (previously +20%).
Maven's Chisel of Procurement now provides +40% Item Rarity at 20% Quality (previously +60%).
Maven's Chisel of Proliferation now provides +10% Monster Pack Size at 20% Quality (previously +20%).
Maven's Chisel of Divination now provide +50% more Divination Cards at 20% Quality (previously +100%).
Maven's Chisel of Scarabs now provides +50% more Scarabs at 20% Quality (previously +100%).
Maven's Chisel of Avarice now provides +50% more Currency at 20% Quality (previously +100%).
Quality applies to Pack Size instead of Item Quantity can no longer appear as a Corruption Implicit Modifier on Maps
Chisels now always apply +20% Quality to Maps.
Cartographer Strongboxes can no longer roll the modifier granting Contained Items additional Quality.
The Empyrean Apparatus Unique Strongbox no longer has a modifier granting Contained Items additional Quality.
Eldritch Altars Influenced by the Searing Exarch can now grant Orbs of Scouring, and Eldritch Altars Influenced by the Eater of Worlds can no longer grant Orbs of Scouring.
Greatly reduced the amount of items dropped from explosive barrels in the Fortress Map. These barrels will now drop a comparable number of items to barrels in other maps.
Reduced the number of monsters in Nightmare Maps to not be greatly higher than regular Maps with a high number of monsters.

Resonating Shrine no longer grants a bonus per power, frenzy and endurance charge, instead now granting an additional maximum power, frenzy and endurance charge. This shrine still grants random charge generation on hit and on kill.
Replenishing Shrine Buff now grants 200% increased Mana Regeneration Rate (instead of Regenerate 10% of Mana per second).
Lesser Replenishing Shrine Buff now grants 100% increased Mana Regeneration Rate (instead of Regenerate 3.33% of Mana per second).
The efficacy of the Ambush Scarab of Discernment has been reduced.
Lowered the bonus Reward granted by the Blight Scarab of Invigoration by 20%.
Blight Scarab of Blooming no longer adds additional non-Boss waves of monsters to each Blight Portal.

Blight Scarab of the Blightheart no longer doubles the number of waves spawned in Blight encounters. It now guarantees each portal has 2 additional waves, with chances for a 3rd and 4th wave to occur.
The Horned Scarab of Awakening now grants Tier 16+ Areas contain the Bosses from a random Maven Invitation.
Introduced the Abyss Scarab of Descending, which Guarantees an Abyssal Depths in an area.
Introduced the Anarchy Scarab of the Exceptional, which adds an additional Exceptional Rogue Exile to the area.
Introduced the Betrayal Scarab of Unbreaking, which gives a chance for Syndicate Members to not lose Ranks when they are Interrogated.

Introduced the Expedition Scarab of Infusion, which makes Logbooks dropped in areas always drop with 4 implicit modifiers, and causes Expedition monsters to gain increased difficulty and reward for each Remnant detonated.

Introduced the Kalguuran Scarab of Enriching, which increases the amount of Ore gained from Ore Deposits and the Difficulty and Reward of monsters guarding Ore Deposits for every completed Ore Deposit in the area.
Introduced The Ritual Scarab of Corpses, which causes Rituals in the area to contain an additional Rare Itemised Corpse monster that will appear in the Favour window after being defeated.
The Beyond Scarab of Resurgence no longer enrages Beyond Bosses on Low Life, and instead summons the Beyond Bosses of other factions when a Beyond Boss is summoned.
The Delirium Scarab of Neuroses now also grants Delirium Reward Types gain +1 to count on defeating Unique Delirium Bosses.
The Domination Scarab of Apparitions can now also spawn Pantheon Shrines in addition to Conqueror ones.

The Influencing Scarab of Conversion has been renamed to the Influencing Scarab of Interference. It no longer grants a chance for maps to drop as Shaper Guardian, Elder Guardian, Conqueror or Unique Synthesised Maps, and now instead spawns a Conqueror, Elder or Shaper Guardian or Synthesis boss to accompany the Map boss. This additional boss is guaranteed to drop a Shaper Guardian, Elder Guardian, Conqueror or Unique Synthesised Map.

The Legion Scarab of Command has been renamed to the Legion Scarab of Treasures, and now grants chests that are broken out a chance for the chests rewards to be applied to the monsters it breaks out, while also making those monsters more difficult and rewarding
The Ritual Scarab of Selectiveness now allows for rerolling 2 additional times instead of 1.

The Torment Scarab now grants a chance for Tormenting Spirits that have Possessed monsters to be released when that monster is killed. The Spirit then immediately dives into the ground, resurfacing with a new rare monster which they again Possess.
The Scarab of Hunted Traitors can no longer be obtained.

The Scarab of Bisection has been split into 2 separate Scarabs, The Scarab of the Sinistral, which grants 100% increased effect of Area Prefix Modifiers, and Area Suffix Modifiers have no effect, and the Scarab of the Dextral, which grants 100% increased effect of Area Suffix Modifiers, and Area Prefix Modifiers have no effect.
The Kalguuran Scarab now grants Area contains 2 additional Ore Deposits (previously 1, Ruthless is unaffected).
The Torment Scarab now grants Area is haunted by 4 additional Tormented Spirits (previously 3).
The Essence Scarab now grants Area contains 3 additional Imprisoned Monsters (previously 2, Ruthless is unaffected).
The Domination Scarab now grants Area contains 3 additional Shrines (previously 2, Ruthless is unaffected).
The Anarchy Scarab now grants Area contains 5 additional Rogue Exiles (previously 4, Ruthless is unaffected).
The Ambush Scarab now grants Area contains 4 additional Strongboxes (previously 3, Ruthless is unaffected).
Added 5 new Map Modifiers that can roll on Originator-influenced and Nightmare Maps.

The following Maps have been added back to the Atlas: Shrine, Fungal Hollow, Pen, Carcass, Lair, Bog, Terrace, Siege, Dungeon, Relic Chambers, Cold River, Coral Ruins, Wharf, Fields, Plaza, Cage, Spider Lair, Courthouse, Graveyard, Gardens, Haunted Mansion, Reef, Dry Sea, Foundry, Dig.

The following Maps have been removed from the Atlas: Arachnid Nest, Colosseum, Waste Pool, Thicket, Academy, Infested Valley, Cells, Desert, Bone Crypt, Toxic Sewer, Precinct, Shore, Spider Forest, Estuary, Channel, Bazaar, Glacier, Villa, Ramparts, Museum, Desert Spring, Temple, Underground River, Plateau, Phantasmagoria.
Drowning Orbs no longer move towards players if there are no other enemies in range of the player.

The following Map Modifiers can no longer roll:
  • Players and their Minions deal no Damage for 3 out of every 10 seconds.
  • Area contains Petrification Statues.
  • Players have reduced Action Speed for each time they've used a Skill Recently.
  • Players are Marked for Death after killing a Rare or Unique monster.
  • Rare and Unique Monsters remove Life, Mana and Energy Shield from Players or their Minions on Hit.
  • When a fifth Impale is inflicted on a Player, Impales are removed to Reflect their Physical Damage multiplied by their remaining Hits to that Player and their Allies within 1.8 metres.
  • Area has patches of Awakeners' Desolation.
  • Player Skills which Throw Traps throw fewer Traps.
  • Players' Minions have less Movement/Attack/Cast Speed.
  • Damage Players' Totems take from Hits is taken from their Summoner's Life instead.
